title: Tasmania Backs Bell Bay as Green Hydrogen Hub with $70M Investment
content: Abel Energyâ€™s Bell Bay Powerfuels Project has been chosen by the Tasmanian Government to establish Tasmaniaâ€™s first large-scale green hydrogen hub in the Bell Bay industrial zone. This $1.2 billion initiative, supported by $70 million in federal funding and major partners like Iberdrola Australia and TasPorts, marks a significant move toward industrial decarbonization and clean fuel production. The project will generate up to 45,000 tonnes of green hydrogen and 300,000 tonnes of green methanol annually, starting in 2028. Utilizing advanced technologies including electrolysis, biomass gasification, and COâ‚‚ capture, the facility will support both domestic use and exports. It is expected to create over 740 jobs and position Bell Bay as a key player in Australiaâ€™s clean energy transition.
author: Analysts Account
